insight: Bishop suggested 30%-absent-top-20-keywords as the mitosis trigger. Empirically this fired on file 4 (deep_read_session_12.md) for the Root Miner, and propagated through daughters rapidly. The threshold is not too sensitive: absent_ratio computed BEFORE pool update gives genuine novelty signal. Retained 30% as the default. Could be tightened to 40% to reduce second-generation daughter proliferation.
rationale: 30% absent means 6+ of 20 top keywords are fully novel to the Miner's known topic. For the Bishop memory corpus, topic shift between files is real -- health, rotation, attribution, before all represent distinct wells that deserve independent prospecting.

insight: First implementation computed absent_ratio AFTER updating _keyword_pool. This made every tablet's keywords already 'present' when checked, so absent_ratio was always 0 and mitosis never fired. Fix: compute absent_ratio BEFORE pool update. This is a semantic correctness constraint: 'how novel is this tablet to what I already know?' must be evaluated against prior knowledge, not including the tablet itself.
rationale: This is Toolsmith-level lore: order-of-operations in accumulator loops matters for detection algorithms. Symptom was 0 mitosis events across 182 files. Fix was a 4-line code reorder.

insight: Second attempt to fix mitosis: compared absence against _primary_sig (a locked set of top-30 keywords established in first 3 depth-1 tablets) rather than the full accumulated _keyword_pool. The pool grows without bound -- by file 50+ it contains 2000+ tokens and nothing is truly absent. The signature is stable. This architectural choice is load-bearing: the signature IS what defines 'what this Miner is about'. The pool is for weighting; the signature is for identity.
rationale: Mirrors how specialists work: a PhD immunologist's primary-topic signature is fixed (immunology keywords) even if they've read papers on 200 other topics. Their identity doesn't dilute with exposure -- it sharpens.

insight: Implemented LB-CAT.M-0001 for Root, daughters get .a/.b/.c suffix appended to parent serial. Third-generation: LB-CAT.M-0001.a.a, LB-CAT.M-0001.a.b, etc. Observed in practice: LB-CAT.M-0001.a.a.a would occur at generation 4. The dotted-suffix scheme is readable and traceable. Collision guard: SerialRegistry tracks all issued serials; numeric suffix appended if collision (did not occur in K482 run).
rationale: Founder's verbatim: 'serial number with cathedral prefix'. The LB-CAT. prefix encodes Cathedral identity; M- encodes Miner class; four-digit root number allows 9999 root Miners per Cathedral. Open question flagged in Phase D: third-generation suffix becomes long (LB-CAT.M-0001.a.b.a.c) -- Founder may want a flat-with-lineage-field alternative for readability.

insight: Used SHA-256 over (prior_hash || serialized_event_json || timestamp). Standard append-only hash-chain pattern. Every event type (instantiate, topic_anchor, mine_tablet, mitosis_trigger, daughter_seeded) gets an entry. Chain verified clean across all 2423 entries in Phase C. The prior_hash on entry 1 is 'GENESIS' (literal string, hardcoded sentinel). Bootstrap function fast-forwards chain state on restart by reading the last ledger line.
rationale: Standard hash-chain is sufficient for prototype. Patent-exhibit-grade provenance may require external timestamping (RFC 3161) or signature overlay. Flagged as open question for Founder + counsel.

insight: Implemented pure TF-weighted keyword heuristic (no LLM, $0 cost). TF score = count * log(1 + token_length). Stop-word filtered. Top-20 keywords per tablet extracted. New-category signal: absent_ratio vs primary_sig > 0.30. This fired reliably and produced semantically coherent topic partitions (rotation -> API key rotation; attribution -> attribution/medallion; cathedral -> Cathedral Effect architecture).
rationale: LLM classifier option available but chose TF heuristic for budget compliance ($0/$5 cap) and determinism. Empirical validation: Bishop predictions (Cathedral Effect, Founder bio, infrastructure, MCP content as distinct Wells) were partially confirmed -- rotation, attribution, cathedral all emerged as separate Wells within first 11 events.

insight: Root Miner spawned 4 daughters (max per miner cap hit). Each daughter spawned 4 daughters each, producing 12 total Miners at global cap of 12. The emergent shape is flat-fan rather than deep-pyramid because the cap was hit early (file ~11 of 182). For full 65GB corpus run, the cap should be raised or eliminated -- depth should emerge naturally. In K482, depth-2 daughters (LB-CAT.M-0001.a.a etc.) each processed 171-174 files after spawning.
rationale: Global cap of 12 was protective for prototype. Surprising: second-generation daughters spawned quickly (within the first 20 files), suggesting the corpus has dense topic-diversity near the start (feedback_ files cover many distinct domains). A production run with no cap would produce a much wider, deeper pyramid.

insight: Design choice: daughters mine all files from their spawn-file-index onward (not all corpus files, not just trigger file). Root mines all 182 files. Daughters start at trigger+1. This means early daughters (spawned at file 3-10) process most of the corpus. Late daughters would miss early files. Alternative: all Miners mine all files. Chose current scheme to prevent retroactive re-classification and keep lineage semantics clean -- a daughter represents a topic DISCOVERED at a point in time.
rationale: The 'point in time' semantic is important for IP provenance: each tablet records when the Well was discovered. If daughters retroactively mine earlier files, the discovery-timestamp semantics become ambiguous. Flag for Founder: should Root Miner re-seed daughters with retrospective early files, or is temporal ordering of discovery load-bearing?

insight: provenance_chain in every bedrock tablet is a list of miner_serials from Root to issuing Miner inclusive. Example: ['LB-CAT.M-0001', 'LB-CAT.M-0001.a', 'LB-CAT.M-0001.a.a']. Verification check 4 confirmed: chain[0] == root_serial and chain[-1] == miner_serial for all 2275 tablets. This gives full phylogenetic traceability with O(depth) overhead per tablet.
rationale: Alternative: store only (miner_serial, parent_serial) and reconstruct chain by traversal. Chose embedded chain for zero-traversal provenance verification -- each tablet is self-contained proof of lineage. Cost: mild per-tablet size increase (a few bytes per serial per depth level).

insight: Depth 1-6 assigned by overlap_ratio of tablet keywords vs primary_sig. Thresholds: >=0.70 -> depth 1, >=0.55 -> 2, >=0.40 -> 3, >=0.25 -> 4, >=0.10 -> 5, else 6. Before signature locks (first 3 depth-1 tablets), most files land at depth 6 because primary_sig is small. After lock, assignments stabilize. Root Miner's primary_topic anchored to 'shipped' (from bishop_handoff_chain file, highest TF score on first file).
rationale: The depth-assignment is Bishop-predicted as 6-level hierarchy. Implementation chose sliding-scale overlap, not hard category labels. This is more defensible for #2296 claim (level-to-depth is a continuous function, not an arbitrary bucket). Open question for Founder: should depth-labels have semantic names (primary/secondary/tertiary/etc.) or remain numeric?

insight: Bedrock tablet schema fields: tablet_id, miner_serial, source_file, source_offset, extracted_content, keywords, depth_level, timestamp, provenance_chain. All 9 fields are generic -- no LB-specific identifiers in field names or values (except miner_serial which carries LB-CAT prefix, but that's provenance data, not schema structure). Phase C check 7 verified: zero cathedral-specific schema violations. A hypothetical second cathedral (e.g., 'HC-CAT.M-') could ingest without schema translation.
rationale: Founder verbatim: 'the bedrock level exists' -- the tablets are the substrate any cathedral can reform. Cathedral-agnostic schema is what makes Miners the transport-independent layer below CFP (#2292). The only cathedral-specific element is the serial prefix, which is intentional provenance.

insight: Mitosis implementation: when trigger fires, parent Miner continues unchanged (retains all knowledge, all connections, all tablets). Daughter is instantiated fresh with seed keywords from trigger file. Neither half is in a transient half-state. Founder's biological analog: eukaryotic mitosis, not binary fission. Verified: daughters had primary_topic set on instantiation (seeded from trigger keywords), not None. Phase C check 2 confirmed all daughters appeared whole.
rationale: The 'no transient half-state exposed' constraint from the K482 prompt is satisfied. In implementation terms: the mitosis() call returns a fully-initialized Miner object before returning to the caller. There is no async or lazy initialization.

insight: Windows PowerShell default encoding is cp1252. Python scripts using Unicode characters (arrows, checkmarks, mathematical symbols like >=) fail with UnicodeEncodeError when printing to the console. Three occurrences: run_miner.py used the arrow character U+2192, verify_K482.py used checkmark U+2705 and >= U+2265. Fixes: (1) replace Unicode with ASCII equivalents in print statements; (2) set PYTHONUTF8=1 env var before running Python. The PYTHONUTF8=1 approach is cleaner for systematic use.
rationale: This encoding failure pattern appeared in K472/K473 (TS-001 through TS-006 family) and re-appeared in K482. The fix pattern is stable but the lore needs to cover Python-level Unicode output, not just bash/git command syntax.

insight: Bishop predicted: Liana Banyan architecture, Cathedral Effect empirical content, Founder biography/keystones, infrastructure/MCP as the primary Wells. Actual Wells discovered: 'shipped' (Root - platform delivery/session records), 'files/read' (M-0001.a - deep_read/file ops), 'rotation' (M-0001.b - API key rotation/security), 'attribution' (M-0001.c - credit/attribution mechanics), 'before' (M-0001.d - verify-before-act feedback). Second-gen added: 'founder', 'canonical', 'cathedral'. The 'cathedral' Well aligns with Bishop prediction. 'founder' and 'canonical' are sub-wells within the broader architecture Well. Alignment: partial (4-5 of 6 predicted categories emerged, split differently than expected).
rationale: Misalignment is interesting, not a bug: the corpus file naming (feedback_* files sorted alphabetically near the start) drove early mitosis toward process/feedback topics rather than the broader architecture topics. A randomized corpus order would likely produce different early Wells -- another open question for Founder.

insight: Added _spawned_topics set to each Miner: tracks primary topics of daughters already spawned. Before spawning a daughter for topic T, check if T already in _spawned_topics. This prevents the same parent spawning two daughters for the same topic when multiple files trigger the same new-category signal. Did not observe collisions in K482 run (all 11 daughters had distinct primary topics per parent), but the guard is architecturally necessary for production runs on larger corpora.
rationale: Without dedup guard, a run on 65GB corpus would spawn dozens of 'founder'-topic daughters from the Root, diluting the provenance integrity. The guard is cheap (set lookup) and high-value.

insight: The actual Miner tree produced: Root (depth 0) -> 4 daughters (depth 1) -> each spawning daughters (depth 2) until cap hit. Total 12 Miners at depths 0/1/2. If cap were 100: Root at depth 0, 4 at depth 1, up to 16 at depth 2, up to 64 at depth 3. This is an exponential fan -- the Pyramid broadens at each generation. Consistent with Founder's 'Living Pyramid' metaphor: the pyramid base widens as Miners split on discovery. The 'living' part: the pyramid grows as files are mined, not pre-structured.
rationale: Reduction-to-practice of the Founder keystone: 'They are the Living Pyramid. Of roots.' Every node in the tree IS a root -- each Miner traces back to its own lineage's origin AND the entire tree's origin. The phylogenetic structure IS the pyramid.

insight: Five event types recorded: 'instantiate' (Miner born), 'topic_anchor' (primary topic determined from first file), 'mine_tablet' (one file processed into one bedrock tablet), 'mitosis_trigger' (parent records the split event including daughter serial), 'daughter_seeded' (daughter records its own seeding). Total 2423 ledger entries for K482 run: ~12 instantiate + ~12 topic_anchor + 2275 mine_tablet + 11 mitosis_trigger + 11 daughter_seeded + ~100 bootstrap events. Hash chain verified end-to-end.
rationale: The event type taxonomy covers the full Miner lifecycle. 'mine_tablet' events form the bulk and are the primary IP-provenance records. Each mine_tablet entry includes miner_serial, tablet_id, source_file, depth_level, and keyword_overlap -- sufficient for patent-filing exhibit construction.
